zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilla-Brigte Ua Maíl Muaid of Cenél Fiachrach於0944年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 奧法利時，他的父親Maolmora Leinster30歲，母親Joan MacMurchada20歲。他和Bridget Giolla至少有1個女兒。他去世於1018年在他的家鄉享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: Ua
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 賈, see   Jia   1. Compare   Kah   2.
West African (Senegal): Tukulor name of unexplained etymology. Compare   Kah   1.

variants.name: Jia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ho, Hong, Li Ping, Tao, Ying, Bei, Hang, Jian, Lei, Aijun, Anning, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 賈, meaning ‘price’ or ‘merchants’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Jia (賈), the name of a state (located in Linpan in Shanxi province) granted by King Kang of Zhou, to Gong Ming, a grandson of King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). After the state was annexed by the state of Jin, the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) from the placename Jia (賈), the name of a fief (located in Linpan in Shanxi province), granted to She, also called Jia Ji (賈季) or Jia Tuo (賈佗), son of a noble called Hu Yan in the state of Jin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Kah
meanings: West African (Gambia): variant of   Ka   2.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 賈, possibly based on its Cantonese pronunciation, see   Jia   1. Compare   Ka   1.
Chinese: possibly from Cantonese form of the Chinese name 嘉 (meaning ‘praise’), which was monosyllabic personal names or part of disyllabic personal names of some early Chinese immigrants in the US.

nameMeanings.name: Maíl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  Scottish:  see  Meil  .   Black  , at Meal, notes that the surname may not have originated in Shetland but derive from the settlement of some Orkney families there in the early 17th century.
2  Welsh, English:  see  Male  .
3  Irish:  see  Malley  .

variants.name: Malley
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Malley  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in northern France called Mailly (Marne, Somme, and elsewhere).
Americanized form of Rusyn (from Slovakia) Mlej (see Malay ).

variants.name: Male
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Mael (literally ‘prince’).

English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French mail ‘mallet, club, mace’, or Middle English (Old French) maille ‘ring (of chain mail), armour’, or Old French maille ‘speckle, stain’, or Middle English maille (Old French maaille) ‘coin worth half a penny’, or from a shortened form of Anglo-Norman French amel, Old French esma(i)l ‘enamel’ (compare Mailer ). Any of these terms might have been used to characterize an individual's behaviour, occupation, or appearance.

English: nickname Middle English (Old French) male ‘bag, pouch’, also ‘stomach, belly’, perhaps given to someone who wore a distinctive pouch or bag or who made such bags (compare the Middle English occupational term malemakere), or to someone with a prominent belly.

variants.name: Black
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Meil
meanings: Scottish (Orkney): habitational name from one of several places in Orkney (and perhaps Shetland) named from Old Norse melr ‘sandbank’. The surname is now spelled Meil in Orkney and Mail in Shetland. The name is rare in Britain and Ireland.
German: variant of   Meile  .

nameMeanings.name: Muaid
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal, Eoin.
Irish (County Limerick): shortened form of   McQuaid  .

variants.name: McQuaid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Colum, Nuala.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaid ‘son of Uad’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Scottish form McQuate .

en.lifeSummary: When Gilla-Brigte Ua Maíl Muaid of Cenél Fiachrach was born in 0944, in County Offaly, Ireland, his father, Maolmora Leinster, was 30 and his mother, Joan MacMurchada, was 20. He had at least 1 daughter with Bridget Giolla. He died in 1018, in his hometown, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gilla-Brigte Ua Maíl Muaid of Cenél Fiachrach est né en 0944, à County Offaly, Irlande, son père, Maolmora Leinster, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Joan MacMurchada, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Bridget Giolla. Il est décédé en 1018, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans.
